---
title: "Lyrics Repeating"
output: html_notebook
---




```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
```

```{r}
#reads in the hot100_top10 csv
hot100_top10 <- read_csv("hot100_top10.csv")
top10_full <- read_csv("top10_full.csv")
```

```{r}
#takes the top10 and seperates the lyrics line by line, then removes the lines where there is nothing in it
lines2 <- top10_full %>%
  separate_rows(lyrics, sep = "\n") %>% filter(lyrics != "")

#takes the lines, then takes the rank, year, artist, title, and lyrics and sorts it. from there it removes when the lyric is only sung once
line_counts2 <- lines2 %>% 
  count(rank, year, artist, title, lyrics, song.length, genre, label, line_repeat_count, title_repeat_count, sort = TRUE) %>% filter(n != 1)
```

```{r}
#takes the hot100_top10 and seperates the lyrics line by line, then removes the lines where there is nothing in it
lines <- hot100_top10 %>%
  separate_rows(lyrics, sep = "\n") %>% filter(lyrics != "")

#takes the lines, then takes the rank, year, artist, title, and lyrics and sorts it. from there it removes when the lyric is only sung once
line_counts <- lines %>% 
  count(rank, year, artist, title, lyrics, sort = TRUE) %>% filter(n != 1)



data_ranked <- line_counts %>%
  arrange(desc(rank))

ggplot(data_ranked, aes(x=rank)) +
  geom_bar() 

```

```{r}
lines2_clean <- lines2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(line_repeat_count))
top_genres <- lines2_clean %>%
  count(genre, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  pull(genre)

lines2_top <- lines2_clean %>%
  filter(genre %in% top_genres)

lines_clean <- lines2_top %>%
  # 1. Remove rows where Genre is NA
  filter(!is.na(genre), genre != "NA") %>% 
  # 2. Combine the two levels into one
  mutate(genre = fct_recode(genre, 
                            "Pop, R&B" = "R&B, pop"))

# Now plot using df_clean
ggplot(lines_clean, aes(x = line_repeat_count, y = genre)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Repetitiveness by Genre (Top Genres)")
```

```{r}


lines_clean2 <- lines_clean %>%
  # remove rows where genre or song_length is missing
  filter(!is.na(genre), genre != "na", !is.na(song.length)) %>%
  # extract just the time (e.g., "6:25") and convert to seconds
  mutate(
    # remove anything in parentheses like "(full version)"
    time_clean = str_extract(song.length, "\\d+:\\d+"),
    # convert "mm:ss" to total seconds
    duration_secs = period_to_seconds(ms(time_clean))
  )

# 3. plot using the new numeric 'duration_secs'
ggplot(lines_clean2, aes(x = duration_secs, y = line_repeat_count)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "blue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red") + 
  labs(
    title = "song length vs. repetitiveness",
    x = "song length (total seconds)",
    y = "line repetition count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
clean_labels <- line_counts2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(label), label != "na") %>%
  mutate(
    # remove anything in parentheses
    label = str_split_i(label, "\\(", 1),
    label = str_trim(label),
    label = str_to_title(label)
  )

# 2. find the names of the top 10 most frequent labels
top_10_names <- clean_labels %>%
  count(label, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  pull(label)

# 3. filter the main data to keep only those 10 names
label_top_10 <- clean_labels %>%
  filter(label %in% top_10_names) %>%
  mutate(label = fct_reorder(label, line_repeat_count, .fun = median))

# 4. plot
ggplot(label_top_10, aes(x = line_repeat_count, y = label)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "blue", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "repetition count: top 10 record labels",
    x = "line repetition count",
    y = "record label"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
